title: Digitaria pedicellaris
intro: Digitaria pedicellaris, also known as finger grass or hairy finger grass, is a type of grass native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Digitaria pedicellaris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and dried extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and as a natural remedy for skin and hair care.
taxonomy: Digitaria pedicellaris belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It is a species of grass, and as such, it is classified in the kingdom Plantae. Within the kingdom, it is further classified into the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Liliopsida, and the order Poales. The order Poales is a group of flowering plants that includes several families of grasses, including Poaceae. Digitaria pedicellaris is a specific species within the family Poaceae.

It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its potential health benefits.
